shower.thought: We spend the first part of our lives learning how to be ourselves, and the second part forgetting.
You've never actually seen your own face—only reflections and photographs of where your face used to be.
Time moves faster as you age not because you experience more, but because each year becomes a smaller fraction of your total life.
The people who know you best are the ones you see most often, but also the ones who know the oldest version of you.
You can't remember being bored as a child, but you were definitely bored—you just called it playing.
Every problem you've ever solved is a problem you'll never remember solving.
The scariest part of ending a friendship isn't the loss—it's realizing how little effort it took for someone to become a stranger again.
You're nostalgic for moments that weren't happy when they happened.
Confidence is just believing your own story more than other people's stories about you.
The longer you know someone, the less you actually see them—you just see the version you've already decided they are.
You only know what you like because you know what you didn't like first.
Sleeping is the one thing humans do every day without ever getting better at it.
The most dangerous lies are the ones you tell yourself while believing you're being honest.
You can't prove you're not a completely different person every time you wake up.
Nostalgia is just grief for a time you were still present for.
Most of your life is spent preparing for moments that disappear the second you stop preparing for them.
You'll never know if someone was truly your best friend or just the person you happened to see the most.
The older you get, the fewer photos you have of yourself—right when you'd want the most evidence that you existed.
Your biggest fear isn't usually what you're afraid of—it's the realization that you've been afraid of it your whole life.
A conversation you thought was forgettable probably meant everything to someone else.

motivational.thought:
Your potential is not determined by your circumstances—it's determined by what you do with them.
— Unknown

The person who moves a mountain begins by carrying away small stones.
— Confucius

Discipline is choosing between what you want now and what you want most.
— Unknown

Every expert was once a beginner who refused to quit.
— Unknown

You cannot control the wind, but you can adjust your sails.
— Dolly Parton

The only thing standing between you and your goal is the story you keep telling yourself about why you can't achieve it.
— Unknown

Progress is not about perfection. It's about direction.
— Unknown

Your life does not get better by chance, it gets better by change.
— Jim Rohn

The regret of not trying will always be greater than the pain of failure.
— Unknown

What lies behind you and what lies before you are tiny matters compared to what lies within you.
— Ralph Waldo Emerson

You become what you repeatedly do. Excellence is not an act but a habit.
— Aristotle

The distance between your dreams and reality is called discipline.
— Unknown

Comparison is the thief of your own progress. Stop measuring yourself against others and start measuring yourself against yesterday.
— Unknown

The best time to plant a tree was 20 years ago. The second best time is today.
— Chinese Proverb

Your future self is watching you right now through memories you haven't created yet.
— Unknown

Success is not final, failure is not fatal. It is the courage to continue that counts.
— Winston Churchill

The only permission you need is your own.
— Unknown

Small daily improvements are the key to staggering long-term results.
— Robin Sharma

Comfort is the enemy of growth. Choose discomfort and watch yourself transform.
— Unknown

You don't have to be perfect to be worthy. You just have to be willing.
— Unknown

darkjoke.thought:
My therapist asked if I ever think about the future.
I said, "Not since the accident."

I told my friend he was being paranoid about his job security.
He said, "Easy for you to say. You're already replaceable."

My dating profile says I'm "emotionally unavailable."
My therapist says that's just honesty in marketing.

I asked the funeral director if business was good.
He said, "Can't complain. People are dying to get in."

My mother-in-law asked when we're having kids.
I said, "We're still workshopping the idea," then went back to ignoring her.

I used to be afraid of commitment.
Now I'm afraid of literally everything else, so commitment seems fine.

The doctor said I need to cut back on alcohol.
I said, "Doc, you're not my real dad."

My best friend got married last week.
I was happy for him. Then I remembered I had to give a speech.

I told my boss I wanted a raise.
He said, "Your job satisfaction isn't tied to money."
I said, "Exactly. Which is why I'm tying my job satisfaction to better employment."

My kid asked why I'm always tired.
I said, "Because parenthood is like a really long funeral where the corpse talks back."

I've been on so many diets, my body doesn't know what I look like anymore.
It's like we're in an open relationship.

My psychiatrist and I have a great relationship.
I say something, he writes it down, I pay him.
It's basically marriage but with eye contact and honesty.

I asked my accountant if I was going to be okay financially.
He said, "Define 'okay.'"
We haven't spoken since.

My ex said she needed someone more mature.
Last I heard, she found him. He had a curfew.

I realized I'm at the age where my back pain has more of a social life than I do.
We go out three times a week. Sometimes four.

My therapist asked about my support system.
I said, "My bed, my couch, and the wall I cry into. They're very supportive."

I told my friend I was seeing someone new.
He said, "That's great!"
I said, "No, literally. There's someone standing behind me right now. I'm very worried."

My doctor said I need to exercise more.
I said, "Doc, getting out of bed is cardio when you're this depressed."

I asked my parents why they never told me I was adopted.
They said, "We were hoping you'd figure it out and leave."

I got my DNA test results back.
Turns out I'm 100% disappointed in my life choices.
The science is irrefutable.
